sources.label: Yahoo Finance - Cambricon 688256.SS news page (no new material headlines Jun 6-9)
Tom's Hardware - Cambricon targets 500,000 AI chips in 2026 (pre-window context: yields ~20%, HBM supply risk)
TrendForce - Cambricon 2026 triple-output rumor faces SMIC limits

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already demoted Cambricon to 117 on this crowding-sensitive CPO alpha list for being fully discovered (now +36% YTD, ~$121B cap) with no direct CPO/photonics leverage; nothing material has emerged since June 6 and today's +1.6% is drift, so no second move is warranted - just mild alpha decay.
verdicts.bull: Q1 2026 revenue grew ~160% and the company is scaling toward ~500K accelerators in 2026 as China's leading Nvidia substitute; domestic AI capex policy tailwinds remain strong.
verdicts.neutral: The localization story is real but fully priced and consensus-owned; at rank 117 the list already treats it as a discovered, indirect-to-CPO name, and nothing since June 6 changes that setup.
verdicts.bear: ~20% yields on flagship Siyuan 590/690, SMIC N+2 capacity limits, and constrained domestic HBM supply mean 2026 output targets can miss badly against a ~$121B valuation built on flawless execution.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Confirmed hyperscaler order volumes or yield/HBM supply breakthroughs validating the 500K-unit 2026 shipment plan (H1 results, supply-chain datapoints).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Production miss from low SMIC yields and HBM scarcity triggering a derating of an extremely crowded, expensively valued localization trade.
